title: Steering Angle Sensor (SAS) Calibration / Zero Point Reset
description: Reset the steering angle sensor zero point after wheel alignment, steering component replacement, or any procedure that disturbs the steering neutral position.
common_symptoms: stability control intervention at wrong times
physical_solution: Reset SAS zero point so stability control systems know where straight-ahead is. Required after alignment or any steering/suspension work.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — $31 routine accessible on most OEMs. Physical preconditions (wheels straight, level surface) critical. Routine IDs vary by OEM.
preconditions: Ignition ON engine running (on most OEMs)

procedure_steps.description: Park on flat level surface. Set steering wheel exactly to straight-ahead position. Do not move wheel during calibration.
Start engine (required on most OEMs for SAS cal).
Poll routine completion: $31 03 [SAS_CAL_ROUTINE_ID]. Confirm positive response.
Clear related DTCs: $14 FF FF FF
Test drive. Turn left and right through full lock. Confirm DSC/ESC light extinguished and stability control operates correctly.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Zero point calibration via Techstream or $31 routine. Physical preconditions strictly enforced.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: EPS/VSA SAS calibration. Level surface and wheel center critical. HDS guided procedure.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: CONSULT III+ SAS cal. Routine accessible via UDS $31 on many platforms.
oem_notes.Ford: SAS cal via IDS. Extended session $10 03 required first. Common after alignment or rack replacement.
oem_notes.GM: Service StabiliTrak calibration procedure. Scan tool routine required after alignment.
oem_notes.BMW: DSC SAS calibration via ISTA. Level surface and wheel center position verified by ECU.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: G85 SAS basic setting via ODIS/VCDS $31. Standard procedure after any steering/chassis work.
oem_notes.Stellantis: ESIM/ESP SAS cal. Pre-2018 straightforward. 2018+ SGW may block — attempt HS-CAN first.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: ESC SAS calibration via GDS. Common after suspension or alignment work.
